title: PLC Workflows MPC Adapter Specification
description: Forge spoke for OT advanced process control. Acts as an APC controller: subscribes to process variables from the hub, computes MPC/APC and plant-wide optimization moves, writes setpoints back to the PLC layer, and emits every control decision as a governed ContextualRecord. Also owns the PLC SDLC (git workflows + CI/CD) pillar.
author: Roger Henley

adapter.name: PLC Workflows MPC Adapter
adapter.description: Advanced process control controller spoke (MPC/APC instantiation, control-strategy selection, plant-wide optimization) plus PLC SDLC tooling. Supervisory/advisory controller for Rockwell Logix PLCs over EtherNet/IP.

data_sources.name: Control Moves
data_sources.description: Manipulated-variable moves computed by the MPC/APC controllers.

data_sources.name: Model Identifications
data_sources.description: Process models identified from operating data (FOPDT/SOPDT/state-space) and their fit quality.

data_sources.name: Strategy Recommendations
data_sources.description: Recommended control strategy (PID/APC/MPC) for a given process.

data_sources.name: Optimization Decisions
data_sources.description: Plant-wide optimization setpoint targets toward a user-defined objective.

data_sources.name: Process Variables
data_sources.description: PV/CV/SP value changes subscribed from the hub (fed by OT/historian spokes).

data_sources.name: Setpoint Write-back
data_sources.description: Setpoint / MV writes pushed back to the PLC layer via the hub (or direct EtherNet/IP).

data_sources.name: Managed Control Loops
data_sources.description: Enumeration of the control loops / controllers this spoke manages.

connection_requirements.name: forge_hub_endpoint
connection_requirements.description: Forge hub gRPC endpoint the spoke registers with.

connection_requirements.name: plc_path
connection_requirements.description: Rockwell Logix EtherNet/IP path 'ip[/slot]' (via pycomm3). Empty = hub-mediated only.

connection_requirements.name: rearm_holdoff_s
connection_requirements.description: Seconds the link must be healthy before the supervisor (re)arms.

connection_requirements.name: heartbeat_timeout_s
connection_requirements.description: PLC heartbeat must advance within this many seconds.

connection_requirements.name: sp_min
connection_requirements.description: Soft lower clamp on the written setpoint (PLC enforces the hard clamp).

connection_requirements.name: sp_max
connection_requirements.description: Soft upper clamp on the written setpoint (PLC enforces the hard clamp).

connection_requirements.name: dry_run
connection_requirements.description: If true, read and solve but never write (commissioning). Default true for safety.

connection_requirements.name: verify_ssl
connection_requirements.description: Verify TLS/SSL certificates for the hub connection.

quality_rules.name: equipment_id_required
quality_rules.rule: Every control record must carry an equipment_id.

quality_rules.name: loop_id_required
quality_rules.rule: Every control record must identify its control loop via context.extra.loop_id.

quality_rules.name: timestamp_preferred
quality_rules.rule: Control events should include a timestamp; defaults to now(utc) if missing.

known_limitations.limitation: The adapter does not yet open hub gRPC streams on its own; the forge hub drives the AdapterBase contract.
known_limitations.mitigation: The adapter implements configure/start/stop/health/collect/write/subscribe/discover; live PLC I/O over EtherNet/IP is wired through the SupervisorRunner attached at start().

known_limitations.limitation: Plant-wide optimization (Phase 4) is an interface-only stub.
known_limitations.mitigation: Phase 1 shipped FOPDT/SOPDT identification + PID/APC/MPC strategy selection. Phase 2 shipped the OSQP-backed LinearMpcController, the SupervisorRunner state machine, and the LogixLink (pycomm3 EtherNet/IP) link, plus live-mode adapter wiring. Phase 3 shipped the L5X ↔ JSON converter + CLI + GitHub Actions workflow generator + PLC-side ST/ladder/tag templates. Phase 4 implements plant-wide optimization; see docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.

decision_quality_value.summary: Governed, auditable advanced process control: every controller move and optimization decision is recorded with full operational context.
examples: Audit every setpoint change made by an MPC controller, with the loop, mode, and objective that drove it.
Correlate control-strategy recommendations with downstream quality and throughput.
Trace plant-wide optimization decisions back to the user-defined objective (e.g. maximize proof gallons).
